# Newsi MVP Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Build the Newsi MVP web app so an authenticated user can save one natural-language interest profile, get one automatically generated in-app digest per day, read it on `Today`, and revisit historical entries in `Archive`.

**Architecture:** Use a single Next.js App Router app with server-rendered pages, Auth.js for authentication, Prisma + PostgreSQL for persistence, and a cron-triggered server route for daily digest generation. Keep the product intentionally narrow: one account timezone, one active interest profile per user, one digest per day, and a thin provider adapter around a single external web-capable LLM.

**Tech Stack:** Next.js, React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Auth.js, Prisma, PostgreSQL, Zod, Vitest, React Testing Library, Playwright

---

## Implementation Notes

- Start from an empty repository.
- Use `pnpm` consistently.
- Target deployment on Vercel or a comparable platform with scheduled HTTP cron support.
- Support one LLM provider in MVP. Implement a provider adapter boundary so the provider can be swapped later without rewriting digest business logic.
- Use Google OAuth for MVP authentication to avoid building password flows or email magic links.
- Store the user timezone once from the browser during Topics save if the account timezone is empty.
- Persist the digest day as a canonical `digestDayKey` string in `YYYY-MM-DD` format, derived from the user's stored timezone. Do not store digest ownership as an arbitrary timestamp.
- Keep UI intentionally editorial and sparse; do not introduce dashboard-style density.

## Data Model Outline

### `User`

- `id`
- `email`
- `name`
- `image`
- `accountTimezone`
- auth adapter fields

### `InterestProfile`

- `id`
- `userId`
- `interestText`
- `firstEligibleDigestDayKey`
- `updatedAt`

Constraint:

- unique `userId`

### `DailyDigest`

- `id`
- `userId`
- `digestDayKey`
- `status` (`scheduled` | `generating` | `failed` | `ready`)
- `title`
- `intro`
- `contentJson`
- `readingTime`
- `retryCount`
- `providerName`
- `providerModel`
- `failureReason`
- `createdAt`
- `updatedAt`

Constraint:

- unique (`userId`, `digestDayKey`)

`contentJson` should store the exact parsed object that passes `digestResponseSchema`, so the cron writer and the Today/Archive renderers share a single contract.

- `DATABASE_URL`

- `AUTH_GOOGLE_ID`
- `AUTH_GOOGLE_SECRET`
- `CRON_SECRET`
- `LLM_API_KEY`
- `LLM_MODEL`
- `APP_URL`

## Task 2: Add Authentication, Prisma, and the Database Schema

**Files:**
- Create: `prisma/schema.prisma`
- Create: `src/lib/db.ts`
- Create: `src/lib/auth.ts`
- Create: `src/app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts`
- Modify: `src/app/page.tsx`
- Modify: `src/app/signin/page.tsx`
- Create: `src/app/(app)/layout.tsx`
- Test: `tests/unit/timezone.test.ts`

- [ ] **Step 1: Define the Prisma schema**

Create `prisma/schema.prisma` with:

```prisma
enum DigestStatus {
  scheduled
  generating
  failed
  ready
}

model User {
  id                String           @id @default(cuid())
  name              String?
  email             String?          @unique
  image             String?
  emailVerified     DateTime?
  accountTimezone   String?
  accounts          Account[]
  sessions          Session[]
  interestProfile   InterestProfile?
  dailyDigests      DailyDigest[]
  createdAt         DateTime         @default(now())
  updatedAt         DateTime         @updatedAt
}

model InterestProfile {
  id                        String   @id @default(cuid())
  userId                    String   @unique
  interestText              String
  firstEligibleDigestDayKey String
  updatedAt                 DateTime @updatedAt
  user                      User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
}

model DailyDigest {
  id            String       @id @default(cuid())
  userId        String
  digestDayKey  String
  status        DigestStatus @default(scheduled)
  title         String?
  intro         String?
  contentJson   Json?
  readingTime   Int?
  providerName  String?
  providerModel String?
  failureReason String?
  createdAt     DateTime     @default(now())
  updatedAt     DateTime     @updatedAt
  user          User         @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@unique([userId, digestDayKey])
}
```

Also add standard Auth.js adapter models `Account`, `Session`, `VerificationToken`.

- [ ] **Step 2: Create the Prisma client and run the first migration**

Create `src/lib/db.ts`:

```ts
import { PrismaClient } from "@prisma/client";

const globalForPrisma = globalThis as unknown as { prisma?: PrismaClient };

export const db =
  globalForPrisma.prisma ??
  new PrismaClient({
    log: process.env.NODE_ENV === "development" ? ["warn", "error"] : ["error"],
  });

if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== "production") globalForPrisma.prisma = db;
```

Run:

```bash
pnpm exec prisma migrate dev --name init_auth_and_digest
```

Expected:

- migration directory created
- Prisma client generated successfully

- [ ] **Step 3: Configure Auth.js with Google OAuth**

Create `src/lib/auth.ts`:

```ts
import NextAuth from "next-auth";
import Google from "next-auth/providers/google";
import { PrismaAdapter } from "@auth/prisma-adapter";
import { db } from "@/lib/db";

export const { handlers, auth, signIn, signOut } = NextAuth({
  adapter: PrismaAdapter(db),
  providers: [Google],
  pages: { signIn: "/signin" },
  session: { strategy: "database" },
});
```

Create `src/app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts`:

```ts
import { handlers } from "@/lib/auth";

export const { GET, POST } = handlers;
```

Modify `src/app/signin/page.tsx` so the page actually starts Google OAuth:

```tsx
import { signIn } from "@/lib/auth";

export default function SignInPage() {

    <main className="flex min-h-screen items-center justify-center bg-stone-950 text-stone-100">
      <div className="w-full max-w-md p-10">
        <p className="mb-4 text-xs uppercase tracking-[0.3em] text-stone-400">
          Personal Daily Synthesis
        </p>
        <h1 className="text-5xl font-semibold">Newsi</h1>
        <form
          action={async () => {
            "use server";
            await signIn("google", { redirectTo: "/today" });
          }}

          <button className="mt-10 bg-white px-4 py-2 text-sm text-stone-950">
            Continue with Google
          </button>
        </form>

}
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Protect the app area with an authenticated layout**

Create `src/app/(app)/layout.tsx`:

```tsx
import { redirect } from "next/navigation";
import { auth } from "@/lib/auth";

export default async function AppLayout({
  children,
}: {
  children: React.ReactNode;
}) {
  const session = await auth();

  if (!session?.user) {
    redirect("/signin");
  }

  return <>{children}</>;
}
```

```tsx

export default function HomePage() {
  redirect("/today");
}
```

- [ ] **Step 5: Add a small timezone utility test to establish the domain test harness**

Create `tests/unit/timezone.test.ts`:

```ts
import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
import { normalizeTimezone } from "@/lib/timezone";

describe("normalizeTimezone", () => {
  it("falls back to UTC for unknown values", () => {
    expect(normalizeTimezone("Mars/Base")).toBe("UTC");
  });
});
```

Create `src/lib/timezone.ts`:

```ts
export function normalizeTimezone(value?: string | null) {
  if (!value) return "UTC";
  try {
    Intl.DateTimeFormat(undefined, { timeZone: value });
    return value;
  } catch {
    return "UTC";
  }
}
```

## Task 4: Implement Topics Save and Edit Flow

**Files:**
- Create: `src/lib/topics/schema.ts`
- Create: `src/lib/topics/service.ts`
- Create: `src/components/topics/topics-form.tsx`
- Create: `src/app/(app)/topics/page.tsx`
- Test: `tests/unit/topics-schema.test.ts`
- Test: `tests/integration/topics-page.test.tsx`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing schema test**

Create `tests/unit/topics-schema.test.ts`:

```ts
import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
import { interestProfileSchema } from "@/lib/topics/schema";

describe("interestProfileSchema", () => {
  it("rejects an empty interest description", () => {
    const result = interestProfileSchema.safeParse({ interestText: "" });
    expect(result.success).toBe(false);
  });
});
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Implement validation and persistence helpers**

Create `src/lib/topics/schema.ts`:

```ts
import { z } from "zod";

export const interestProfileSchema = z.object({
  interestText: z.string().trim().min(2).max(1000),
  browserTimezone: z.string().trim().optional(),
});
```

Create `src/lib/topics/service.ts`:

```ts
import { db } from "@/lib/db";
import {
  getDigestDayKey,
  getNextDigestDayKey,
  hasDailyRunPassed,
  normalizeTimezone,
} from "@/lib/timezone";
import { interestProfileSchema } from "@/lib/topics/schema";

export async function saveInterestProfile(userId: string, input: unknown) {
  const data = interestProfileSchema.parse(input);
  const user = await db.user.findUniqueOrThrow({ where: { id: userId } });
  const timezone = user.accountTimezone ?? normalizeTimezone(data.browserTimezone);
  const now = new Date();
  const firstEligibleDigestDayKey = hasDailyRunPassed(timezone, now)
    ? getNextDigestDayKey(timezone, now)
    : getDigestDayKey(timezone, now);

  await db.interestProfile.upsert({
    where: { userId },
    update: { interestText: data.interestText },
    create: {
      userId,
      interestText: data.interestText,
      firstEligibleDigestDayKey,
    },
  });

  if (!user.accountTimezone) {
    await db.user.update({
      where: { id: userId },
      data: { accountTimezone: timezone },
    });
  }
}
```

Important:

- Only set `firstEligibleDigestDayKey` on first profile creation.
- Later edits must update `interestText` only and must not move the eligibility boundary.

- [ ] **Step 4: Implement the Topics form and page**

Create `src/components/topics/topics-form.tsx`:

```tsx
"use client";

import { useEffect, useState } from "react";

export function TopicsForm({
  initialValue,
  onSubmitAction,
}: {
  initialValue: string;
  onSubmitAction: (formData: FormData) => Promise<void>;
}) {
  const [timezone, setTimezone] = useState("UTC");

  useEffect(() => {
    setTimezone(Intl.DateTimeFormat().resolvedOptions().timeZone || "UTC");
  }, []);

  return (
    <form action={onSubmitAction} className="mx-auto max-w-3xl px-12 py-20">
      <h1 className="text-5xl font-semibold">What are you exploring?</h1>
      <p className="mt-4 max-w-2xl text-stone-500">
        Describe your interests in plain language. Newsi will use this as your standing brief for future daily digests.
      </p>
      <input type="hidden" name="browserTimezone" value={timezone} />
      <textarea
        name="interestText"
        defaultValue={initialValue}
        className="mt-12 min-h-[220px] w-full border-none bg-transparent text-lg leading-8 outline-none"
        placeholder="Describe your interests in plain language..."
      />
      <button className="mt-10 bg-stone-950 px-4 py-2 text-sm text-white">
        Save interests
      </button>
    </form>
  );
}
```

Create `src/app/(app)/topics/page.tsx` using a server action that:

- loads the current session
- loads the current interest profile
- calls `saveInterestProfile`
- revalidates `/topics` and `/today`
- renders copy that tells first-time users the first guaranteed digest appears after the next local 07:00 run if they saved after today's run

## Task 5: Implement Digest Schema, Prompting, and the Daily Generation Service

**Files:**
- Create: `src/lib/digest/schema.ts`
- Create: `src/lib/digest/prompt.ts`
- Create: `src/lib/digest/provider.ts`
- Create: `src/lib/digest/service.ts`
- Test: `tests/unit/digest-schema.test.ts`
- Test: `tests/unit/digest-service.test.ts`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing digest schema test**

Create `tests/unit/digest-schema.test.ts`:

```ts
import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
import { digestResponseSchema } from "@/lib/digest/schema";

describe("digestResponseSchema", () => {
  it("requires 3 to 5 sections", () => {
    const result = digestResponseSchema.safeParse({
      title: "Today's Synthesis",
      intro: "A short intro",
      sections: [],
      readingTime: 5,
    });

    expect(result.success).toBe(false);
  });
});
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Implement the digest schema and prompt builder**

Create `src/lib/digest/schema.ts`:

```ts
import { z } from "zod";

export const digestResponseSchema = z.object({
  title: z.string().min(1),
  intro: z.string().min(1),
  readingTime: z.number().int().min(3).max(12),
  sections: z.array(
    z.object({
      title: z.string().min(1),
      summary: z.array(z.string().min(1)).min(2).max(4),
      keyPoints: z.array(z.string().min(1)).min(2).max(5),
      whyItMatters: z.string().min(1).optional(),
    }),
  ).min(3).max(5),
});
```

Create `src/lib/digest/prompt.ts`:

```ts
export function buildDigestPrompt({
  dateLabel,
  interestText,
}: {
  dateLabel: string;
  interestText: string;
}) {
  return `
You are generating a personal daily synthesis for a knowledge worker.

Date: ${dateLabel}
Standing brief: ${interestText}

Requirements:
- Focus on what changed today or very recently
- Produce 3 to 5 thematic sections
- Keep the tone calm and editorial
- Avoid generic filler
- Return structured JSON only
`;
}
```

import { describe, expect, it, vi } from "vitest";
import { generateDigest } from "@/lib/digest/service";

describe("generateDigest", () => {
  it("returns parsed digest data from the provider", async () => {
    const provider = {
      generate: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({
        title: "Today's Synthesis",
        intro: "Signals across your tracked space moved in two clear directions.",
        readingTime: 6,
        sections: [
          { title: "AI", summary: ["a", "b"], keyPoints: ["c", "d"] },
          { title: "Tools", summary: ["a", "b"], keyPoints: ["c", "d"] },
          { title: "Startups", summary: ["a", "b"], keyPoints: ["c", "d"] },
        ],
      }),
    };

      provider,
      dateLabel: "2026-03-21",
      interestText: "AI agents and design tools",
    });

    expect(result.title).toBe("Today's Synthesis");
    expect(provider.generate).toHaveBeenCalledOnce();

});

- [ ] **Step 4: Implement the provider interface and digest service**

Create `src/lib/digest/provider.ts`:

```ts
import OpenAI from "openai";
import { digestResponseSchema } from "@/lib/digest/schema";
import { z } from "zod";

type DigestResponse = z.infer<typeof digestResponseSchema>;

export interface DigestProvider {
  generate(input: { prompt: string }): Promise<DigestResponse>;
}

export function parseDigestResult(raw: unknown): DigestResponse {
  return digestResponseSchema.parse(raw);
}

export function createOpenAiDigestProvider(): DigestProvider {
  const client = new OpenAI({ apiKey: process.env.LLM_API_KEY });

  return {
    async generate({ prompt }) {
      const response = await client.responses.create({
        model: process.env.LLM_MODEL!,
        input: prompt,
        tools: [{ type: "web_search_preview" }],
        text: {
          format: {
            type: "json_schema",
            name: "newsi_digest",
            schema: {
              type: "object",
              additionalProperties: false,
              properties: {
                title: { type: "string" },
                intro: { type: "string" },
                readingTime: { type: "number" },
                sections: {
                  type: "array",
                  minItems: 3,
                  maxItems: 5,
                  items: {
                    type: "object",
                    additionalProperties: false,
                    properties: {
                      title: { type: "string" },
                      summary: { type: "array", items: { type: "string" } },
                      keyPoints: { type: "array", items: { type: "string" } },
                      whyItMatters: { type: "string" },
                    },
                    required: ["title", "summary", "keyPoints"],
                  },
                },
              },
              required: ["title", "intro", "readingTime", "sections"],
            },
          },
        },
      });

      const json = JSON.parse(response.output_text);
      return parseDigestResult(json);
    },
  };
}
```

Create `src/lib/digest/service.ts`:

```ts
import { buildDigestPrompt } from "@/lib/digest/prompt";
import {
  createOpenAiDigestProvider,
  type DigestProvider,
} from "@/lib/digest/provider";

export async function generateDigest({
  provider = createOpenAiDigestProvider(),
  dateLabel,
  interestText,
}: {
  provider?: DigestProvider;
  dateLabel: string;
  interestText: string;
}) {
  const prompt = buildDigestPrompt({ dateLabel, interestText });
  return provider.generate({ prompt });
}
```

## Task 6: Implement the Daily Digest Scheduler and Persistence Flow

**Files:**
- Create: `src/app/api/cron/digests/route.ts`
- Modify: `src/lib/timezone.ts`
- Modify: `src/lib/digest/service.ts`
- Create: `vercel.json`
- Test: `tests/unit/timezone.test.ts`

- [ ] **Step 1: Extend timezone utilities with digest-day logic**

Update `tests/unit/timezone.test.ts`:

```ts
it("returns the digest day in the user's timezone", () => {
  expect(getDigestDayKey("Asia/Shanghai", new Date("2026-03-21T00:30:00+08:00"))).toBe("2026-03-21");
});

it("moves the first eligible digest day to tomorrow after the 07:00 cutoff", () => {
  expect(getNextDigestDayKey("Asia/Shanghai", new Date("2026-03-21T08:00:00+08:00"))).toBe("2026-03-22");
});
```

Update `src/lib/timezone.ts`:

```ts
import { addDays } from "date-fns";
import { formatInTimeZone, toZonedTime } from "date-fns-tz";

export function getDigestDayKey(timezone: string, now = new Date()) {
  return formatInTimeZone(now, timezone, "yyyy-MM-dd");
}

export function shouldRunDigestNow(timezone: string, now = new Date()) {
  return Number(formatInTimeZone(now, timezone, "HH")) >= 7;
}

export function hasDailyRunPassed(timezone: string, now = new Date()) {
  return Number(formatInTimeZone(now, timezone, "HH")) >= 7;
}

export function getNextDigestDayKey(timezone: string, now = new Date()) {
  const zonedNow = toZonedTime(now, timezone);
  return formatInTimeZone(addDays(zonedNow, 1), timezone, "yyyy-MM-dd");
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Implement the cron route**

Create `src/app/api/cron/digests/route.ts` with logic to:

- verify `Authorization: Bearer ${CRON_SECRET}`
- query users that have both `accountTimezone` and `interestProfile`
- select users whose local hour is `07` or later, but still on the same local digest day
- derive `digestDayKey = getDigestDayKey(user.accountTimezone, now)` for every read and write
- skip the user if `digestDayKey < interestProfile.firstEligibleDigestDayKey`
- load any existing digest row for that exact `digestDayKey`
- if no row exists, create one with `scheduled`
- if a row exists with `ready`, skip it
- if a row exists with `scheduled`, resume it
- if a row exists with `failed` and `retryCount >= 3`, treat it as terminal and skip it
- if a row exists with `generating` and `updatedAt` is older than 15 minutes, treat it as stale and resume it
- atomically claim the row by updating status to `generating` inside a transaction or optimistic `updateMany` guard
- call `generateDigest`
- write `title`, `intro`, `contentJson`, `readingTime`, `status=ready`
- on error, retry until the stored `retryCount` reaches 3 total attempts for that digest day, then mark `failed`

Use this structure:

```ts
export async function GET(request: Request) {
  const authHeader = request.headers.get("authorization");
  if (authHeader !== `Bearer ${process.env.CRON_SECRET}`) {
    return new Response("Unauthorized", { status: 401 });
  }

  // load eligible users, generate digests, persist results
  return Response.json({ ok: true });
}
```

Inside the per-user worker, use an explicit retry loop:

```ts
const existing = await db.dailyDigest.findUnique({
  where: { userId_digestDayKey: { userId: user.id, digestDayKey } },
});

if (digestDayKey < interestProfile.firstEligibleDigestDayKey) return;
if (existing?.status === "ready") return;
if (existing?.status === "failed" && existing.retryCount >= 3) return;

const claimed = await db.dailyDigest.updateMany({
  where: {
    userId,
    digestDayKey,
    status: { in: ["scheduled", "failed"] },
  },
  data: { status: "generating" },
});

if (claimed.count === 0 && existing?.status !== "generating") return;

const startingAttempt = (existing?.retryCount ?? 0) + 1;

for (let attempt = startingAttempt; attempt <= 3; attempt += 1) {
  try {
    // mark generating, call provider, persist ready result, break
    break;
  } catch (error) {
    // update retryCount = attempt
    if (attempt === 3) {
      // persist failed state and failureReason
    }
  }
}
```

This keeps retries compatible with the hourly cron schedule because the total retry budget is persisted on the digest row and capped at 3 attempts across all invocations for the same digest day. Later same-day cron runs can recover `scheduled` or stale `generating` rows, but must not revive a terminal `failed` row whose retry budget is exhausted.

- [ ] **Step 3: Add retry bookkeeping to the data model**

```prisma
retryCount Int @default(0)
```

Run:

```bash
pnpm exec prisma migrate dev --name add_digest_retry_count
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Add deployment cron configuration**

Create `vercel.json`:

```json
{
  "crons": [
    {
      "path": "/api/cron/digests",
      "schedule": "0 * * * *"
    }
  ]
}
```

Run hourly, and let the route decide which user timezones are currently at 07:00. This avoids one cron entry per timezone.

- [ ] **Step 2: Implement the digest renderer and archive list**

Create `src/components/digest/digest-view.tsx`:

```tsx
type DigestSection = {
  title: string;
  summary: string[];
  keyPoints: string[];
  whyItMatters?: string;
};

export function DigestView({
  title,
  intro,
  sections,
}: {
  title: string;
  intro: string;
  sections: DigestSection[];
}) {
  return (
    <article className="mx-auto max-w-3xl px-12 py-20">
      <h1 className="text-5xl font-semibold">{title}</h1>
      <p className="mt-6 text-lg leading-8 text-stone-600">{intro}</p>
      <div className="mt-16 space-y-16">
        {sections.map((section) => (
          <section key={section.title}>
            <h2 className="text-2xl font-semibold">{section.title}</h2>
            <div className="mt-6 space-y-4 text-base leading-8 text-stone-700">
              {section.summary.map((paragraph) => (
                <p key={paragraph}>{paragraph}</p>
              ))}
            </div>
            <ul className="mt-6 space-y-3">
              {section.keyPoints.map((point) => (
                <li key={point}>{point}</li>
              ))}
            </ul>
          </section>
        ))}
      </div>
    </article>
  );
}
```

Create `src/components/archive/archive-list.tsx`:

```tsx
import Link from "next/link";

export function ArchiveList({
  items,
}: {
  items: { digestDayKey: string; title: string; readingTime: number }[];
}) {
  return (
    <div className="mx-auto max-w-4xl px-12 py-20">
      {items.map((item) => (
        <Link
          key={item.digestDayKey}
          href={`/archive/${item.digestDayKey}`}
          className="grid grid-cols-[120px_1fr_80px] border-b border-stone-200 py-5 text-sm"
        >
          <span>{item.digestDayKey}</span>
          <span>{item.title}</span>
          <span>{item.readingTime} min</span>
        </Link>
      ))}
    </div>
  );
}
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Implement Today page state branching**

Create `src/app/(app)/today/page.tsx` so it:

- loads the authenticated user
- loads the interest profile
- derives today's `digestDayKey` from `accountTimezone`
- reads the current digest entry
- renders:
  - `EmptyState` if no interest profile
  - `StatusPanel` for `scheduled`, `generating`, `failed`
  - `DigestView` for `ready`

Use this decision table:

```ts
if (!interestProfile) -> EmptyState("What are you exploring?", ...)
if (!digest) -> StatusPanel("Scheduled", "Your first guaranteed digest will appear after the next local 07:00 run.")
if (digest.status === "scheduled") -> StatusPanel("Scheduled", ...)
if (digest.status === "generating") -> StatusPanel("Generating", ...)
if (digest.status === "failed") -> StatusPanel("Retrying", ...)
if (digest.status === "ready") -> DigestView(...)
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Implement Archive pages**

Create:

- `src/app/(app)/archive/page.tsx`
- `src/app/(app)/archive/[digestDayKey]/page.tsx`

Behavior:

- archive index shows all digests newest first, regardless of `ready`, `scheduled`, `generating`, or `failed`
- detail page loads the selected digest by `digestDayKey`
- detail page renders `DigestView` for `ready` entries
- detail page renders `StatusPanel` for `scheduled`, `generating`, and `failed` entries so history stays accessible even when generation did not complete
- detail page returns `notFound()` only when no row exists for that `digestDayKey`
